1.background_text: Imperial Prison - three hours before execution
1.bubble_layout_plan: The Crown line is a supernatural thought received by Elianor. Place the thought bubble near Elianor but leave her face visible; use small dots or glow pointing to her forehead, eye reflection, or the invisible crown source.

dialogue.speaker: Eclipse Crown
dialogue.text: Wake, little sovereign. The blade is still three hours away.

1.lettering_plan: Use the accepted Eclipse Gothic style: black ornamental narration card, luminous ivory Crown thought bubble, elegant serif lettering with high contrast.
1.visual_continuity_out: The next panel must begin from this lower edge instead of jumping to a disconnected shot.

2.bubble_layout_plan: Elianor's line belongs to her mouth/face; the Crown reply must feel like a reflection or inner voice from her eye.

dialogue.speaker: Elianor
dialogue.text: I died.

dialogue.speaker: Eclipse Crown
dialogue.text: Briefly. Inefficiently.

2.image_prompt_addendum: Do not start as an isolated eye close-up. Start by continuing vertically from the bottom of panel 1: show Elianor's lower body, bruised legs, bare feet or prison slippers, torn white dress hem, chains, wet stone floor, and strands of black hair. Then transition the viewer's gaze upward or into an inset/reflection close-up of her eye where the Crown is visible. The panel should feel like the camera panned downward from panel 1 before moving into the eye beat.
2.lettering_plan: Keep the same black ornamental narration card and ivory thought/speech bubble system.
2.visual_continuity_in: Direct continuation from panel 1 bottom edge. Reveal the cropped lower body and feet so the vertical scroll does not feel abruptly cut.
2.visual_continuity_out: End on the eye/reflection motif so the following panel can move into Elianor pushing herself upright.

dialogue.speaker: Elianor
dialogue.text: Then I still have time.

3.visual_continuity_in: Continue from panel 2: Elianor's eye reflection or silver glint becomes the next visual focus.
3.visual_continuity_out: Her posture begins low and rises, supporting the chapter's survival arc.

4.visual_continuity_out: Quiet foreshadowing panel; symbol is visual only and not readable script.

dialogue.speaker: Caelan
dialogue.text: No. Not again.

5.visual_continuity_out: Correct body continuity: bare hand while waking, gloves nearby but not worn.

dialogue.speaker: Caelan
dialogue.text: Saddle the black relay. Wake no herald who cannot lie.

6.visual_continuity_out: Transition from private memory to public duke identity with gloves and coat.

dialogue.speaker: Maerith
dialogue.text: Let every loyal heart witness the moon's correction.

dialogue.speaker: Varrien
dialogue.text: A lawful death leaves no ghosts.

dialogue.speaker: Luceren
dialogue.text: Then let it be lawful.

7.visual_continuity_out: Establish antagonist motives and limits through placement: priest at rite, emperor at law-seat, prince apart.

dialogue.speaker: Selene
dialogue.text: Your Highness... look left when the bell rings.

dialogue.speaker: Elianor
dialogue.text: You should not be here.

8.visual_continuity_out: Adds human reaction and a route from prison to plaza before the scaffold.

dialogue.speaker: Maerith
dialogue.text: Confess, child, and the crowd may remember you gently.

dialogue.speaker: Elianor
dialogue.text: I will not make your fear holy.

9.visual_continuity_out: Builds direct confrontation before magic; keep Elianor standing.

dialogue.speaker: Varrien
dialogue.text: Elianor Veyr, the empire offers one mercy: kneel.

dialogue.speaker: Elianor
dialogue.text: The empire has mistaken kneeling for truth.

10.visual_continuity_out: Court hierarchy reads vertically from emperor above to Elianor below.

dialogue.speaker: Eclipse Crown
dialogue.text: Let me eat.

dialogue.speaker: Elianor
dialogue.text: Not all of it. Not me.

11.visual_continuity_out: Magic escalates but Crown remains reflection/shadow-based; reaction beat before rescue.

dialogue.speaker: Caelan
dialogue.text: I contest the verdict because Princess Elianor Veyr was murdered once already.

12.visual_continuity_out: End with vertical power reversal: duke kneels below, princess standing above, emperor exposed above them.

panels.image_prompt: Tall vertical opening panel, prison ceiling dissolving into a barred view of the chained moon, Elianor Veyr lying on cold stone in torn white execution dress, bruised wrists, long black hair spread like ink, faint crown shape only reflected in one ash-gray eye, original dark royal fantasy manhwa, no readable text

panels.narration: The moon was still chained when Elianor opened her eyes.
panels.top_anchor: Chained moon behind prison bars, gothic ironwork, black sky.
panels.bottom_anchor: Elianor's body continues downward out of frame: torn white dress, bruised legs partly cropped, prison floor, loose hair, chains dragging toward the bottom edge.

panels.narration: Terror asked her to kneel. Pride answered first.

panels.narration: Among the imperial seals was an older mark: a crescent over a sword, from a law the palace had tried to bury.

panels.narration: Far north of the scaffold, another death woke with her.
panels.visual_continuity_out: Correct body continuity: bare hand while waking, gloves nearby but not worn.

panels.narration: He had arrived late once. His body remembered the cost before his mind finished screaming.

panels.narration: The rite needed fear. The emperor needed law. The prince needed distance.

panels.narration: She saw the contradiction at last: a tyrant who could kill her only if the world agreed to call it justice.

panels.narration: The Crown opened its hunger like a second sky, and Elianor held the leash with both hands.
panels.visual_continuity_out: Magic escalates but Crown remains reflection/shadow-based; reaction beat before rescue.

panels.image_prompt: Final cliffhanger panel, Caelan kneeling at base of scaffold beneath northern banner, black ducal coat and fur-lined cape, gloved hand over heart, eyebrow scar visible, Elianor above him stunned but upright, Varrien rigid on dais, Maerith's beads frozen mid-prayer, chained moon looming overhead, no readable banners
panels.narration: The plaza forgot how to breathe.
